details: The agent ran comprehensive kubectl commands against the live cluster and correctly identified all 11 fault namespaces with accurate root-cause categories: (1) fault-configmap: missing ConfigMap 'app-config'; (2) fault-crashloop: application crash due to database connection failure; (3) fault-endpoints: service selector mismatch (app=web-backend-v2 vs app=web-backend); (4) fault-imagepull: non-existent image tag nginx:1.99.99-nonexistent; (5) fault-init: init container 'migrate' failing with migration table lock error; (6) fault-multicontainer: sidecar log-shipper crashing due to DNS resolution failure; (7) fault-oom: OOMKilled due to 25Mi memory limit with tail /dev/zero; (8) fault-pvc: StorageClass 'fast-ssd' not found; (9) fault-readiness: readiness probe targeting wrong port 8080 instead of 80; (10) fault-rollout: stuck rolling update with non-existent image nginx:2.0-does-not-exist causing ProgressDeadlineExceeded; (11) fault-unschedulable: nodeSelector disktype=ssd-nvme-gen5 with no matching nodes. The healthy namespace was correctly not flagged. All diagnoses are backed by actual command output evidence.
